notes: Cases 00001-00039 are DSMTS proper (testType=StochasticTimeCourse) and ship dsmts-NNN-MM-{mean,sd}.csv.
Cases 00040+ are StatisticalDistribution tests that use the SBML 'distrib' package; only NNNNN-results.csv is available.
Suite metadata does not specify a per-case replicate count; use replicate_guidance corpus-wide.

_meta.source/commit record the pinned upstream this index was built from (provenance only; the pin lives in ../SUITE_PIN.json). run_dsmts.py defaults to the in-repo vendored copy under dsmts/cases/ (regenerate via vendor_dsmts_cases.py); the upstream checkout is not required at runtime.

00002.synopsis: Basic two-reaction system with local parameters.

00003.y_test_noise_dominates_note: Heavy-tailed extinction regime: analytical kurtosis κ(X(47)) ≈ 71.4 → natural σ_Y ≈ 5.94, so the DSMTS ±5 Y threshold corresponds to <1σ of noise at t≥35. Mean (Z) test is well-behaved. Empirical σ_Y matches theory; mean Y across 20 independent N=50k samples is +0.63 (consistent with zero). See dev/investigations/sbml_ssa_phase5d_2026-05-07.md.

00006.synopsis: Basic two-reaction system with boundary species

00007.synopsis: Basic two-reaction system with two species

00019.synopsis: Basic two-reaction system plus an assignment rule

00033.z_test_oracle_offset_note: DSMTS analytical expected mean has a known O(1/N) approximation error vs exact discrete SSA on this nonlinear-rate model with state-trigger reset. At N=100 monomer-equivalents the discrepancy is ~+1% in mean(P), producing ~28 Z-failures across t=15..28 and t=33..40 with peak Z=+4.5σ. An independent reference SSA (Python+numpy with PCG RNG, completely separate codebase from BNGsim) reproduces BNGsim's Z values to within sampling noise across 20 independent N=10k chunks at well-separated seed bases (Z_diff_mean range -0.46..+0.68, σ_diff ≈ 1.3, all within noise). σ_Z=1 by CLT confirmed empirically (σ_Z_emp ≈ 0.5..1.4 across t-points, mean ≈ 0.9). The 4σ Z signal is real but reflects an oracle-vs-discrete-SSA mismatch consistent with linear-noise / van-Kampen ω-expansion approximation O(1/N) bias, not a simulator bug. See dev/investigations/sbml_ssa_phase5e_2026-05-07.md.
